req.title: Create encounter (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
preconditions: role:clinician
patient exists

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can create an Encounter with valid start/end timestamps (positive), and that the API rejects requests where end < start with HTTP 422 (negative). Boundary cases cover end == start (same instant). Additional negative paths cover missing mandatory fields, unauthorized roles, and conflicting state transitions. The violation in test[5] was that a negative kind test used heuristic=STC, which is reserved for state-transition coverage (a structural/positive heuristic). The fix changes test[5] heuristic from STC to NEG, which is the correct heuristic for negative/error-path tests. All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role:clinician
A patient record exists (patient_id provided by fixture)
start timestamp is strictly before end timestamp
actions: POST /Encounter with resourceType, subject, status, period.start, period.end where start < end
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field
Response body period.start matches request value
Response body period.end matches request value
trace: R-HLT-056: clinician shall create an Encounter with start and end timestamps
R-HLT-056: encounter stored as expected outcome
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps(api_client, clinician_auth_headers, existing_patient_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "in-progress",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        }
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["period"]["start"] == "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    assert body["period"]["end"] == "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role:clinician
A patient record exists
end timestamp is strictly before start timestamp
actions: POST /Encounter with period.end set to a datetime earlier than period.start
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a diagnostic or issue message referencing invalid period
trace: R-HLT-056: end before start shall be rejected
R-HLT-056: error pathway HTTP 422 when end < start

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_boundary
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role:clinician
A patient record exists
end timestamp equals start timestamp (zero-duration encounter)
actions: POST /Encounter with period.start == period.end (same ISO-8601 instant)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created OR HTTP 422 depending on server policy for zero-duration encounters
If 201: response body contains id and matching period values
If 422: response body contains a diagnostic message
trace: R-HLT-056: boundary at end == start (adjacent partition between valid and invalid)
R-HLT-056: BVA on the period.end < period.start predicate

tests.name: test_create_encounter_missing_mandatory_period_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role:clinician
A patient record exists
period field is omitted entirely from the request body
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a diagnostic message referencing missing period or start timestamp
trace: R-HLT-056: missing mandatory elements must be tested
R-HLT-056: DT row where period=absent maps to rejection

tests.name: test_create_encounter_unauthorized_role_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with role:patient (not clinician)
A patient record exists
Valid start and end timestamps provided
actions: POST /Encounter with valid payload but using patient-role auth headers
expected: Encounter is NOT stored in the system
trace: R-HLT-056: permission denial must be tested

tests.name: test_create_encounter_conflicting_state_transition_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role:clinician
An Encounter already exists for the same patient with status:finished
Attempt to POST a new Encounter referencing a finished/closed context that the server treats as a state conflict
actions: POST /Encounter with status:in-progress targeting a patient whose active encounter context is finished
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict or HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body contains a diagnostic message referencing state conflict
trace: R-HLT-056: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-056: negative path for conflicting state transition rejection
